title: MD
biography: Raymond Damadian was an American physician and medical practitioner who was the first to propose and demonstrate that nuclear magnetic resonance could be used to detect disease, leading to the development of MRI scanning. He built the first whole-body MRI scanner ('Indomitable') and performed the first human MRI scan in 1977.
aiSummary: Raymond Damadian was a radiology pioneer who proposed using NMR for disease detection and built the first MRI scanner. Performed first human MRI scan in 1977. Based in New York, United States.
techniquesInvented: Concept of MRI for disease detection

citations.title: Tumor detection by nuclear magnetic resonance

awards: National Medal of Technology and Innovation (1988)

timeline.title: Proposed NMR for cancer detection
timeline.description: Published in Science that cancerous tissue differs from normal tissue in NMR signal characteristics

timeline.title: Filed first MRI patent
timeline.description: Patented the concept of using NMR to scan the human body for disease

timeline.title: First human MRI scan
timeline.description: Performed the first whole-body MRI scan on a human using the 'Indomitable' scanner
